fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 72 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when `len(recent) == limit` the function incorrectly allows the request instead of denying it.

constraints.intent: When len(recent) equals limit, the branch must be taken (denied); the condition should be >= not >.
After the if-branch is skipped (else path), recent count must be strictly less than limit for the call to be legitimately allowed.
Verify that when len(recent) equals limit the subsequent denial logic fires correctly (boundary condition check).
If the call is allowed (True), the number of recent requests must be strictly less than the limit.
